wain_guo.name: Wain Guo
wain_guo.summary:
Hailing from Missouri, Wain Guo has claimed a championship, currently competing at the U19 level, a moment that separates contenders from champions. With 7 entries across 4 tournaments, the competitive foundation is built — and more titles are within reach.

Wain is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

In doubles, Wain has formed effective partnerships:
  • Katherine Y Li (current partner) — 2 events, 1 title together in XD

Wain is peaking at the right time in 2022-2023, with 1 title across 2 tournaments — a clear sign that Wain is still ascending.

wanhan_sun.name: Wanhan Sun
wanhan_sun.summary:
Representing Illinois, Wanhan Sun is a tenacious competitor with 3 quarterfinal-or-better finishes, currently competing at the U19 level, building a strong foundation across 4 tournaments. Deeper runs are on the horizon.

Wanhan is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

Wanhan has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

warren_wenxuan_huang.summary:
Out of Illinois, Warren Wenxuan Huang is relentlessly knocking on the door, currently competing at the U13 level, reaching 3 finals across 11 entries. A player who reaches that many championship matches has all the tools — the breakthrough title is coming.

Notably, Warren has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Warren especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #2, a doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Warren has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, NW, Warren isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

wayne_chien.name: Wayne Chien
wayne_chien.summary:
Hailing from New York, Wayne Chien has claimed a championship, currently competing at the U15 level, a moment that separates contenders from champions. With 72 entries across 27 tournaments, the competitive foundation is built — and more titles are within reach.

Notably, Wayne has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Wayne especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #4, 1 doubles title, a mixed doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (5 entries).

With 17 different doubles partners over the years, Wayne adapts to anyone:
  • Steven Li (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in BD (7 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Chingpei Lin (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (4 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Daniel Yan (current partner) — currently teaming up in BD (2 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Rithvika Santhakumar (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (2 events, across 2 seasons)

Wayne is peaking at the right time in 2025-2026, with 1 title across 5 tournaments — a clear sign that Wayne is still ascending.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Wayne has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Wayne has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 1 title, Wayne is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

wenlu_she.name: Wenlu She
wenlu_she.summary:
Wenlu She is among the elite few to reach the podium at the U.S. Selection Event — the tournament that determines who represents America internationally. A top-4 finish at Selection places Wenlu in the rarest company in U.S. junior badminton.

Wenlu is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (3 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

What makes Wenlu especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a doubles best of #3, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event top-4 (U17 GD) • Junior Nationals top-4 (GD U19) • ORC top-2 finisher.

With 12 different doubles partners over the years, Wenlu adapts to anyone:
  • Michael Xu (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in U19/XD (3 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Harshita Kandambakkam (current partner) — currently teaming up in U19 (2 events, across 2 seasons)
  • Ved Vedere — a trusted U19/XD partner (4 events, best: #5, across 2 seasons)
  • Lilian Shu — a trusted GD/U17 partner (6 events, best: #3, spanning 3 seasons)

Wenlu has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Wenlu has competed across 4 different regions (NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience, Wenlu brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

william_geng.name: William Geng
william_geng.summary:
Out of Washington, William Geng has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U15 XD, U15 BS, U15 BD, U13 BD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 14 career titles across 28 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

William is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, William competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 2 singles titles, 5 doubles titles, 6 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U15 XD, U15 BS, U15 BD, U13 BD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals champion (BD U15, XD U15) • 4 ORC titles.

The statistics paint a vivid picture — William boasts a clutch 71% win rate in three-game matches (12/17), a biggest win margin of 33 points, a commanding 77% win rate across 74 matches.

With 16 different doubles partners over the years, William adapts to anyone:
  • Joey Liu (current primary partner) — 10 events together this season alone with 3 titles, building serious momentum (10 total events in BD/U15, across 2 seasons)
  • Erica Chen Lin (current primary partner) — 4 events together this season alone with 2 titles, building serious momentum (4 total events in XD, across 2 seasons)
  • Stephanie Jiayi Song (current partner) — an ongoing partnership that keeps producing results: 4 titles across 5 events in XD, spanning 4 seasons

A veteran of the junior circuit, William has competed across U11, U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

William has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 15 seeded entries across the career, William consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 5 seasons and 14 titles, William has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

William has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Over 6 seasons and 1 title, William has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

william_zhu.name: William Zhu
william_zhu.summary:
A New York competitor, William Zhu is just getting started on the junior circuit with 2 tournament entries. Every champion's story has a beginning, and this is William's.

William competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

willie_wanli_yang.name: Willie WANLI Yang
willie_wanli_yang.summary:
A California competitor, Willie WANLI Yang has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-4), currently competing at the U19 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Willie's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

Notably, Willie has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Willie is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #3, a doubles best of #2, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries) • ORC top-4 finisher.

With 12 different doubles partners over the years, Willie adapts to anyone:
  • Chang He (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (4 events, best: #5, across 2 seasons)

Having moved up to U19, Willie is navigating tougher competition at the higher age group. The adjustment is natural — and with the pedigree Willie brings, a return to the top is a matter of when, not if.

Willie has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Willie has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Willie's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

wilson_chen.name: Wilson Chen
wilson_chen.summary:
Hailing from Oregon, Wilson Chen is a consistent semifinalist, currently competing at the U15 level, reaching the final four 4 times. That kind of reliability at the top of the draw is the signature of a player who will soon be contending for titles.

Notably, Wilson has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Wilson especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #4, a doubles best of #4, a mixed doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Wilson has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 6 different players across the career:
  • Bob Lang (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in BD (4 events, best: #4, across 2 seasons)
  • Spriha Garg — 2 events in XD, best: #3, across 2 seasons

Wilson has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With tournaments spanning NE, NW, NorCal, Wilson isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience, Wilson brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

wufan_zhang.summary:
